description: The figure displays a ball-and-stick crystal structure model of Fe-Mn-Al alloys with B2 ordered structure (space group Pm-3m), representing the cubic supercell used in first-principles DFT calculations. The model visualizes a 2×2×2 supercell or unit cell with atoms depicted as spheres: purple spheres represent Fe atoms (or Fe/Mn shared lattice sites), green spheres represent Al atoms, and the distinct blue/gray sphere indicates Mn substitution at specific Fe sites, illustrating the solid solution formation across the Fe1-xMnxAl composition series. This structural representation supports the computational investigation of chemical stability, electronic density of states, and mechanical properties including elastic constants, bulk modulus, and Debye temperature variations with Mn content. The B2 structure features a CsCl-type arrangement where Al occupies one simple cubic sublattice while Fe and Mn statistically occupy the other sublattice. The visualization provides the geometric foundation for understanding atomic coordination, bond character (mixed covalent-metallic), and structural anisotropy that underpins the reported mechanical behavior and thermodynamic stability trends from FeAl to MnAl.
